articles.title: LAUNCH FASTER WITH AI BUILT FOR MARKETERS 🤠
articles.summary: 👀 Is this familiar? You've got more campaigns to launch than ever—but no new resources are in sight.

articles.title: CONSUMER SENTIMENT FALLS 6%, HIT BY DECLINING STOCKS, HIGHER GAS PRICES
articles.summary: Consumer sentiment fell 6% to its lowest level this year as the Iran war pushed up gas prices and slashed stock values. Short-term economic outlooks dropped 14%, and personal finance expectations fell 10%. Gasoline prices and stock market losses hit middle- and upper-income households hardest. Sentiment could worsen if the conflict persists, with headline inflation expected to reach 4.2% and growth slowing to 2% this year.

articles.title: FROM IMPRESSIONS TO IMPACT: HOW THE OPEN INTERNET CREATES REAL VALUE
articles.summary: Online advertising outside platforms like Google and Meta is shifting from buying impressions to driving measurable outcomes. As fragmentation and data loss weaken old models, optimization is moving upstream to filter better inventory before auctions. At the same time, creative is becoming a key performance signal through engagement data. AI connects media and performance into a continuous loop, allowing campaigns to improve in real-time and making the open web more competitive with walled gardens.

articles.title: EMAIL NURTURE SEQUENCE FRAMEWORK
articles.summary: This framework builds a B2B email nurture sequence by defining audience, story, problem, value, action, and post-nurture experience. Use storytelling and content that solves the problem (templates, frameworks, or case studies). Segment by problem, job, vertical, or company size, and send from a real human, responding to replies. Treat each email as an experiment. Test content, CTAs, format, and incentives, then iterate. Include exclusive assets and use post-nurture actions like demos, roundtables, or trials to drive engagement.

articles.title: MARRIOTT BETS ON REAL-TIME CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE TO INDUSTRIALISE INTIMACY
articles.summary: Marriott is shifting its loyalty strategy from points to emotional connection through better guest experiences. With 270M Bonvoy members and nearly 10K properties, it is investing in real-time feedback during stays instead of post stay surveys. In-app check-in surveys let teams fix issues before they escalate. The goal is to position Marriott as a lifelong travel partner across the full journey, from inspiration to post stay engagement.

articles.title: STORYCHIEF: THE COMMAND CENTER FOR HIGH-PERFORMING TEAMS
articles.summary: Most AI content sounds the same. StoryChief helps your team stand out and drive traffic.

articles.title: THE CXO NEWSLETTER PLAYBOOK: WHY 95% FAIL (AND THE 5-PROMPT STRATEGY SYSTEM)
articles.summary: Most CXO newsletters fail because they skip validation and publish generic content that drives no pipeline. Traditional channels are weakening, with cold email reply rates at 3-5% and LinkedIn reach at 1.6%. Niche newsletters can reach 30-45% open rates and build compounding relationships when they target a clear CXO persona and deliver useful insights. Even 200-300 subscribers can drive 15-20 qualified meetings a year. This piece includes a 5 prompt system to test demand, competition, ROI, and fit before investing.

articles.title: BLUESKY BUILT A NEW AI TOOL THAT WANTS TO FREE YOU FROM SOCIAL ALGORITHMS
articles.summary: Bluesky's Attie is an AI assistant that lets users create custom social media feeds through conversational commands rather than relying on platform-controlled algorithms. The app runs on Bluesky's AT Protocol and pulls data from across the entire ecosystem to build personalized timelines instantly. Attie represents a deliberate contrast to how major platforms use AI primarily to increase engagement and data collection. The long-term vision includes allowing users to build complete social applications without coding knowledge.

articles.title: THE SCIENCE OF WHAT AI ACTUALLY REWARDS
articles.summary: AI citation behavior varies by industry, not a single formula. Many SEO tactics don't scale. A clear opening line boosts citations by about 14%, while hedging hurts performance across all seven verticals. Including a date and a number consistently helps. Price mentions reduce citations in most verticals, and Knowledge Graph entities show negative impact. Heading structure varies widely, from 5 to 9 in crypto to 20 plus in SaaS. Corporate content drives 94.7% of citations, while UGC ranges from 0.5% in finance to 9.2% in crypto.

articles.title: WHY YOUR PRICING CHANGES TAKE QUARTERS INSTEAD OF HOURS
articles.summary: Pricing changes at most SaaS companies take a full quarter of engineering time because access rules and billing logic are hard-coded. AI makes this worse as usage shifts more often. The fix is a four-layer monetization setup that separates access from enforcement, so non-engineers can manage plans and experiments. Superhuman waited six years to raise prices by $10, and even with 4% churn, saw a 28% overnight revenue lift that compounds over time.

articles.title: 7 TYPES OF ZERO-PARTY DATA YOU CAN COLLECT
articles.summary: Shopping intent, experience level, and category interest are all underused in e-commerce.

articles.title: GOOGLE ADS EXPANDING LOYALTY PROGRAM
articles.summary: Google now highlights loyalty perks like member discounts and shipping benefits directly in product listings.

articles.title: UGC LOOP FOR AI PRODUCTS
articles.summary: AI products grow through a loop where users create and share AI work, inspiring others to join and keep the cycle going.
